Nursing Notes:
Patient is a 32-year-old female oil expeller from Mumbai Suburban, Maharashtra, currently married, with middle education. She speaks English and Marathi. She reports a sudden onset of throbbing headache, described as 8/10 in intensity, associated with nausea but no vomiting. Denies any visual changes, neck stiffness, or weakness in limbs. No history of similar episodes. Patient appears anxious and is repeatedly checking her pulse. She has a known history of hypertension, diagnosed 6 months ago. She reports compliance with her medications, which she keeps in her purse. No known allergies. Last meal was 4 hours ago. No recent travel or sick contacts. Patient is alert and oriented x3.
